channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical News
channel.description: Global geopolitical news headlines curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Germany Suspects Russia Behind Signal Phishing Attacks on Politicians
item.description: German government suspects Russia is behind phishing attacks on Signal accounts targeting high-ranking politicians, including two ministers, military personnel and journalists. Around 300 accounts in the political sphere were compromised through fake security chatbot messages.

item.title: Russian Drone Strike Wounds 14 in Odesa, Ukraine Strikes Kill 2 in Kherson
item.description: A Russian drone attack before dawn wounded 14 people including two children in Odesa, while Ukrainian drone strikes killed two people in Russia-occupied Kherson. Zelenskyy says Russia has fired 1,900 attack drones at Ukraine over the past week.

item.title: Global Military Spending Hits Record $2.9 Trillion as Europe Surges
item.description: Global military spending reached a new record of almost $2.9 trillion in 2025, the 11th consecutive year of growth. European expenditure surged 14% to $864 billion - the highest level ever recorded for the continent, with Germany crossing 2% of GDP for the first time since 1990.

item.title: China Expands Economic Toolkit During Trade Truce With US
item.description: China has used its trade truce with the United States to broaden its legal leverage, supply chain and critical technology controls, and expand its toolkit of retaliatory economic measures ahead of a summit between the nations next month.

item.title: Fertiliser Price Surge Threatens Global Grain Harvests
item.description: Farmers worldwide face a second surge in fertiliser prices in four years due to the Iran war. With grain prices too low to cushion the blow from the deeper supply crunch, many are rethinking planting plans, putting global food production at risk.

item.title: Merz Suggests Ukraine May Accept Territorial Loss for EU Path
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said Ukraine may need to accept that some territory remains outside Kyiv's control in a future peace deal with Russia, linking such concessions to EU membership prospects.

item.title: Iran Submits Proposal to Reopen Strait of Hormuz Without Nuclear Deal
item.description: Iran handed the US a proposal through Pakistani mediators to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and end the war, postponing nuclear negotiations for later. The move comes as US-Iran talks remain stalled.

item.title: Moody's Upgrades China Economic Outlook to Stable
item.description: Moody's changed its outlook on China's economy from negative to stable, citing resilience despite global headwinds from the Iran war and domestic challenges.

item.title: Netanyahu's Biggest Rivals Join Forces for Israel's Next Election
item.description: Two of Benjamin Netanyahu's top rivals announced they would join forces in the next Israeli election, presenting a unified challenge to the long-serving prime minister.

item.title: Starmer, Trump Discuss Urgent Need to Restore Hormuz Shipping
item.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer and US President Donald Trump discussed the urgent need to restore shipping through the Strait of Hormuz amid the ongoing Iran conflict.

item.title: Israel Reportedly Equipped UAE With Iron Dome During Iran War
item.description: Israel reportedly deployed Iron Dome systems and troops to the UAE during the Iran war, deepening defense cooperation between the two nations.

item.title: Hezbollah Chief Rejects Lebanon-Israel Direct Talks
item.description: Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem rejected Lebanon's planned direct talks with Israel, outlining five points for any solution while vowing to confront Israeli actions.

item.title: Global Defense Spending Reaches Record as Europe Surges 14%
item.description: Global military spending hit a record $2.9 trillion in 2025 with Europe seeing the fastest increase since 1953 as countries respond to the Ukraine war and Iran conflict.

item.title: Taiwan Receives Final Batch of M1A2T Abrams Tanks From US
item.description: Taiwan received the final batch of M1A2T Abrams tanks from the United States, strengthening its defensive capabilities amid rising tensions with China.

item.title: North Korea Opens Memorial Museum for Troops Killed in Ukraine
item.description: North Korea inaugurated a memorial museum in Pyongyang honoring thousands of its troops killed fighting alongside Russia in Ukraine, deepening military ties between Pyongyang and Moscow.

item.title: Kim Jong Un Vows Continued Support for Russia at War Memorial
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un vowed to continue supporting Russia as he opened a memorial museum honoring North Korean troops killed fighting in Ukraine.

item.title: US Becomes OPEC's Swing Producer as Iran War Disrupts Oil Markets
item.description: The United States has stepped in to shield the global economy from the oil crunch triggered by the Iran war, cementing its transformation into the world's dominant energy superpower.

item.title: Gulf Equities Nudge Higher Despite Stalled Iran Diplomacy
item.description: Most Gulf equity markets edged higher despite stalled diplomacy between the US and Iran, as regional investors looked past the ongoing conflict.

item.title: Beijing Blocks Meta's $2B Acquisition of Manus After Security Review
item.description: Chinese regulators blocked Meta's proposed $2 billion acquisition of the AI startup Manus after a national security review, highlighting continued tech tensions.

item.title: Sanctions Escalation Hits Chinese Refiners Tied to Iranian Crude Trade
item.description: Chinese refiners with ties to Iranian crude trade are facing increased pressure from sanctions enforcement as the US-Iran conflict continues.

item.title: China Delivers Record LNG Carrier as Shipbuilding Rivalry With South Korea Intensifies
item.description: China delivered a record-breaking LNG carrier as competition with South Korea in the shipbuilding industry intensifies amid growing demand for energy transport.

item.title: Iran's Foreign Minister Blames US for Failed Talks After Landing in Russia
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi blamed the US for the failure of peace talks after landing in Russia for meetings with President Putin as diplomacy remains stalled.

item.title: DeepSeek's New AI Model Underwhelms Markets Amid Rapid Industry Change
item.description: DeepSeek's latest AI model failed to impress markets as the Chinese company struggles to maintain momentum in a fast-changing artificial intelligence industry.

item.title: Gunfire Persists in Mali Town After Attacks, UN Urges Response
item.description: Gunfire continued in a Malian town after deadly attacks, with the UN calling for an international response to the worsening security situation.

item.title: Iran Suspends Steel Exports as Conflict Targets Industry
item.description: Iran banned exports of steel slabs and sheets until May 30 as the country's steel industry faces strikes during the conflict with Israel and the United States.
